skill.name: startup-metrics-framework
skill.description: Guides founders and operators through startup metrics, including SaaS, marketplace, consumer, B2B, and fundraising metrics.
skill.summary: Build practical startup metrics dashboards and investor-ready metric reviews.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these were false positives caused by markdown formula code fences, metric acronyms, benchmark text, and inline file references; no executable code, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection text was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Content
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged markdown formulas and inline reference paths as external commands, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance. Review showed the content is educational startup metric documentation, not executable code or operational instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are plain markdown formulas, metric labels, benchmark text, or resource references. I found no executable scripts, shell commands, network calls, or secret handling in the scanned files.

content.value_statement: Founders often track too many metrics or the wrong metrics for their stage. This skill helps choose, calculate, benchmark, and present startup metrics for operations and fundraising.
actual_capabilities: Explains core revenue metrics such as MRR, ARR, growth rate, CAC, LTV, and payback period.
Maps metric priorities to startup stages from pre-seed through Series B and later.
Provides SaaS, marketplace, consumer, mobile, and B2B metric frameworks.
Summarizes benchmark ranges for retention, growth, burn multiple, and sales efficiency.
Helps structure investor-facing metric updates with current values, trends, and context.
limitations: It does not connect to analytics, billing, CRM, or warehouse systems.
It provides benchmark guidance, not financial, legal, or investment advice.
It requires accurate business inputs before calculations can be trusted.
It does not include automated spreadsheet models or dashboard templates.

use_cases.title: Choose Seed-Stage Operating Metrics
use_cases.description: Select a focused set of metrics for product-market fit, retention, runway, and early revenue growth.

use_cases.title: Review SaaS Unit Economics
use_cases.description: Calculate CAC, LTV, payback period, net dollar retention, magic number, and burn multiple.

use_cases.title: Prepare Investor Metric Updates
use_cases.description: Create a clear board or fundraising summary with trends, benchmarks, and context for key metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Pick Core Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: We are a [business model] startup at [stage]. Help us choose 5 to 7 core metrics to track weekly and monthly.

prompt_templates.title: Calculate Unit Economics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these inputs to calculate CAC, LTV, LTV:CAC, CAC payback, and burn multiple: [paste inputs]. Explain what each result means.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Growth Efficiency
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these metrics for growth efficiency and retention risk: [paste metrics]. Identify strengths, weak points, and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Build Investor Metrics Narrative
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these startup metrics into an investor-ready narrative for a [round type] fundraise. Include benchmarks, caveats, and priority improvements.

output_examples.input: A Series A SaaS company has $3M ARR, 112% NDR, 92% logo retention, 4.0x LTV:CAC, and 18 months runway.
output_examples.output: The company shows strong retention and healthy unit economics. The update should emphasize efficient growth, durable customer value, and a clear runway plan.

output_examples.input: A marketplace has strong demand growth but declining fill rate and lower repeat transactions.
output_examples.output: The likely issue is marketplace liquidity. The review should focus on supply growth, time to transaction, fill rate, and repeat rate by segment.

output_examples.input: A seed startup tracks total users, downloads, and page views but has weak retention data.
output_examples.output: The metric set is too focused on vanity metrics. Replace it with retention, activation, engagement, conversion, burn rate, and qualitative feedback.

best_practices: Track a small set of metrics that match the company stage and business model.
Pair every metric with a trend, benchmark, segment, and operating decision.
Review core metrics weekly and prepare broader board metrics monthly or quarterly.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize for vanity metrics without retention or revenue context.
Do not compare metrics against benchmarks from a different business model.
Do not present single-period results without trend data or caveats.

faq.question: Which startup stages does this skill support?
faq.answer: It covers pre-seed, seed, Series A, Series B, and later-stage metric priorities.

faq.question: Does it calculate SaaS metrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains MRR, ARR, CAC, LTV, retention, payback period, magic number, and Rule of 40.

faq.question: Can it help with marketplace metrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers GMV, take rate, liquidity, fill rate, repeat rate, and supply-demand balance.

faq.question: Does it replace a finance model?
faq.answer: No. It provides metric guidance and interpretation, but it does not build a full financial model.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide business model, stage, revenue, customer counts, churn, acquisition spend, gross margin, cash balance, and burn rate.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It is educational operating guidance and should be reviewed with qualified finance or legal advisors when needed.
